mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero (0.11% as of Jan 2015), discount rates applied to equity cash flows remain compressed, supporting continued risk asset appreciation. Persistently low policy rates mechanically lower the equity risk premium hurdle, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns as long as rates stay near the zero lower bound.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month average of nonfarm payroll gains accelerates above 250,000, it signals robust labor demand and consumer income growth that gets rapidly priced into equities over the following week, producing a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns immediately following strong employment prints.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average PAYEMS net gain exceeds 250,000 jobs
failure_condition: Post-trigger 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.05), or hit rate of positive returns after trigger falls below 55% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive trailing 3-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, which supports equity valuations over a quarterly horizon as forward earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month INDPRO growth and forward 60d returns stays below 0.03 across two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer-led demand strength that is not visible from either series alone: rising RSAFS combined with elevated HOUST indicates households are both spending and investing in durable housing, amplifying the positive read-through to forward equity returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month YoY growth exceeds 3% AND HOUST 3-month average exceeds 1,000 thousand units
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods produce forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from (or below) unconditional baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: As UNRATE falls below 5.8%, the labor market approaches full employment, raising market expectations of imminent Fed tightening. This anticipation of higher future discount rates produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as rate-hike risk gets priced in.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative-direction signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL YoY inflation falls below 1% while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near the zero lower bound, markets interpret the combination as evidence of weak aggregate demand that monetary policy cannot easily counter, producing below-average forward equity returns despite nominally accommodative rates, over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY inflation below 1% AND FEDFUNDS below 0.25%
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns during joint-trigger periods are not significantly lower than unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign across two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rise in UNRATE (3-month average up more than 0.3pp) combined with a deceleration in PAYEMS gains (3-month average below 100,000) signals an emerging labor market downturn analogous to early recession dynamics, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as growth expectations deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month average rises more than 0.3pp AND PAYEMS 3-month average gain falls below 100,000
failure_condition: Rank IC between the joint UNRATE/PAYEMS signal and forward 20d returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or triggered instances show positive returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects broad economic expansion that supports corporate revenue and earnings growth, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as analysts revise earnings estimates upward in line with realized output growth.
failure_condition: Rank IC between quarterly GDPC1 growth and forward 60d SPX returns remains below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows, or the relationship reverses sign after data revision to GDPC1.
